response.justification: Bogdan brings relevant strengths for this role — active hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks in his current position directly addresses the AI-native environment requirement, and his Sociomantic tenure demonstrates real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience. TypeScript is solid throughout his career, and a consistent software craftsmanship ethos (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) signals the ownership mindset the role demands. The critical gap is Python: it does not appear anywhere in his resume, skills list, or project descriptions, yet the role lists it as a must-have with 'strong' proficiency required. Additionally, his distributed systems depth is now roughly six years stale and was conducted primarily in DLang rather than the Python/TypeScript stack this role centers on. His current Staff title is brand new (April 2025), so the strategic IC track record at that level is thin.
strengths: Active AI-agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq provides direct, current exposure to the agentic engineering environment the role is built around
Proven high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong TypeScript across multiple roles combined with a long software-craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, design patterns, workshops)
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume and skills list, yet the role explicitly requires strong Python as a must-have
Distributed systems depth is dated (~2018–2020) and was built primarily in DLang, not the Python/TypeScript stack expected here
Staff-level strategic IC experience is very limited — the current Staff role started April 2025 and prior Staff titles at Altom were from 2016–2018

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems experience recency and depth
